event: Dieppe, Study No. 2, Facade of Saint-Jacques Tower
symbols: rose window — eternity and the radiance of divine light
Gothic tower — aspiration toward heaven and the transcendent
church in the city — presence of the sacred amid ordinary life
description: A loose, atmospheric pencil and wash sketch on tan-toned paper depicting the facade of the Saint-Jacques Tower in Dieppe, France. The Gothic church dominates the center with its ornate tracery, a prominent rose window, and pointed arched portal. Surrounding buildings crowd the composition on both sides. A few lightly sketched pedestrian figures appear in the foreground street. The drawing is impressionistic in technique, with rapid gestural lines and minimal detail, capturing the overall mass and mood of the architecture rather than precise features.
